title: Find Relief with These 8 Herbal Tinctures for an Upset Stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, which can calm nausea and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
Flavonoids are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby soothing and calming the stomach.
Saponins are good for an upset stomach because they help to soothe and protect the lining of the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by digestive issues.
Triterpenoids are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of nausea and digestive discomfort.

Glycosides, also known as turmeric, are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a liquid extract from the roots of the licorice plant. It has been used for centuries to help with digestive issues, including an upset stomach. The main active ingredient in this tincture is glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken, glycyrrhizin helps to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like gastritis or stomach ulcers, where the stomach lining is inflamed. Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture can also help to reduce nausea and vomiting, and may even help to calm stomach spasms. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, this tincture can help to restore the stomach's natural function and prevent future upset. It can be taken alone or in combination with other herbs to address specific digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Curcuma longa herbal tincture that helps with an upset stomach, you'll need dried turmeric roots or the root powder, a glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a clean workspace. Start by measuring out one part of the turmeric root or powder and mixing it with one part of the solvent in the glass jar. You can adjust the ratio of turmeric to solvent to your liking, but this is a good starting point. Next, store the mixture in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the flavors mix. This process allows the active compounds in the turmeric to infuse into the solvent, creating a potent tincture. After the steeping period, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. You now have a Curcuma longa herbal tincture that you can take by mouth as needed to help soothe an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and soothe the digestive system by blocking pain receptors and improving digestion.
Shogaol is good for an upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system by blocking the production of chemicals that cause stomach pain and discomfort.
Gingerols are good for an upset stomach because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea and vomiting.

Shogaols are good for upset stomach because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract, allowing for faster healing and relief from stomach discomfort.
Zingerone helps an upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy for upset stomach. The tincture is made from the root of the ginger plant and contains compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system. One of the main reasons ginger tincture is good for upset stomach is that it reduces nausea and vomiting. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help ease digestive discomfort and relieve cramps. When taken internally, the active compounds in ginger tincture, such as gingerol and shogaol, work quickly to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This makes ginger tincture an effective remedy for stomach upset caused by food poisoning, motion sickness, or other digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tincture for an upset stomach, you will need fresh or dried turmeric root, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass dropper bottle. First, finely chop the turmeric root into small pieces to release its active compounds. Fill a clean glass jar about one-third full with the chopped turmeric, and then cover it with the carrier oil. Store this mixture in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the turmeric infuse into the oil. After two weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the dropper bottle, discarding the solids. Label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take a few drops in water or as directed by a healthcare professional to help soothe an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for an upset stomach because it helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, making digestion smoother and less painful.

Foeniculin is good for an upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce irritation and inflammation in the digestive system, allowing the stomach to heal and recover naturally.

Polyphenols are good for upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach, making it easier to digest food and relieve symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Phenolic acids are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system, thereby relieving symptoms of nausea, bloating, and stomach pain.
Coumarins are good for upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm down the digestive system and reduce irritation in the stomach lining.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help calm an upset stomach. The tincture, which is made from the dried seeds of the plant, contains a compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the stomach and intestines. When taken in small amounts, fennel tincture can help ease digestive discomfort, such as cramps, bloating, and gas, by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines. This can be especially helpful for people who experience nausea and vomiting, as it can help settle the stomach and prevent further irritation. Fennel tincture may also help to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation, making it a popular natural remedy for a range of stomach-related issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tincture that helps with upset stomach, start by gathering the necessary materials: dried Curcuma longa root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a strainer or cheesecloth. Begin by measuring out one part of dried Curcuma longa root and adding it to the glass jar, then fill the jar with four parts of the solvent, making sure the root is completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the roots release their medicinal properties into the solvent. After the steeping process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in the fridge to prolong its shelf life. When you're ready to use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and take it as needed to help soothe an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal tinctures for upset stomach

Tannins are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food and reducing stomach pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint tincture, is a natural remedy that can help soothe an upset stomach. The tincture is made from peppermint leaves, which contain a chemical called menthol. When you take peppermint tincture, the menthol helps to calm the muscles in your stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping. This can bring quick relief from nausea, heartburn, and indigestion. The menthol in peppermint tincture also helps to slow down digestion, which can be helpful if you're experiencing diarrhea or stomach pain after eating. Additionally, peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach and intestines. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing stomach pain or discomfort due to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Curcuma longa herbal tincture to help with an upset stomach, start by gathering high-quality dried Curcuma longa roots, also known as turmeric. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a dark glass bottle for storing the tincture. First, combine 1 part of the dried Curcuma longa roots with 2-3 parts of the carrier oil in the glass jar. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their active compounds into the oil. After the steeping time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help soothe an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal tinctures for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Carvone is good for an upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive system, which can ease cramps and discomfort.

Limonene is good for an upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines.

remedies.remedy_desc: Carum carvi tincture, also known as caraway seed tincture, is often used to calm an upset stomach. The seeds of the caraway plant contain a natural compound called limonene, which helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. When taken as a tincture, it can ease symptoms of indigestion and nausea by slowing down digestion and reducing the production of stomach acid. This can provide relief from heartburn and bloating, making it easier to digest food. Additionally, caraway seeds have been traditionally used to help with gas and cramps in the stomach, as they contain compounds that help to break down gas and ease muscle spasms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tincture for an upset stomach, you will need dried Curcuma longa root, a glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass dropper bottle. Start by measuring out a handful of dried Curcuma longa root and placing it in the glass jar. Pour enough carrier oil over the root to cover it completely, and then seal the jar with the lid. Place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the root infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the clean glass dropper bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting Curcuma longa tincture can be taken in small amounts, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe an upset stomach. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tincture is often used to help soothe an upset stomach. The tincture is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree, which is a natural source of quinine. The tincture can also help to calm nausea and vomiting, which can be symptoms of an irritated stomach. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tinctures that help with upset stomach, you will need fresh or dried turmeric roots, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, prepare the turmeric roots by washing them with water and chopping them into small pieces. Next, fill the glass jar about 1/4 full with the chopped turmeric roots. Then, add a carrier oil to completely cover the roots, making sure that they are fully submerged. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the roots release their active compounds into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If desired, add a solvent like vodka or glycerin to the oil to help extract the active compounds more efficiently. Finally, label and store the tincture in a cool, dark place, ready to use as needed to soothe an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is a liquid extract made from the Aloe vera plant. It's been used for a long time to help with upset stomachs. The tincture works by soothing and calming the digestive system. It contains a compound called aloin, which helps to slow down the movement of food through the digestive tract. This can help to prevent nausea and vomiting. It also contains other compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can cause stomach pain and discomfort. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis tincture has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections in the digestive system. When taken, the tincture coats the stomach lining, protecting it from acid and other irritants. This can help to reduce stomach acid and prevent heartburn. The tincture can be taken as needed to help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa tincture for upset stomach, first, you'll need to gather the required materials: dried turmeric roots or rhizomes, a glass jar with a lid, vodka or glycerin as a solvent, and a cheesecloth or coffee filter. Start by filling the jar with the dried turmeric, making sure to cover the entire surface. Next, pour the solvent over the turmeric, making sure that the roots are completely covered. If using vodka, a general ratio is 1 part turmeric to 2 parts vodka. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the tincture infuse. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You now have a concentrated turmeric tincture that can be taken as needed for upset stomach relief, typically 20-30 drops mixed with water or taken under the tongue.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria that can cause stomach problems, and it also slows down the movement of food through the digestive system, allowing it to be fully digested.

Tannins are good for upset stomach because they help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, and also have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, which helps to ease digestive issues and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tincture is made from the roots of the turmeric plant, which is a natural remedy for an upset stomach. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the digestive system. When you have an upset stomach, curcumin works to calm down the digestive tract and prevent further irritation. It also helps to reduce the production of stomach acid, which can cause heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, curcumin has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off bacteria and other infections that can cause stomach upset. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Curcuma longa tincture can help to soothe an upset stomach and promote healing. It's often used to treat digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and diarrhea. Curcuma longa tincture can also help to regulate bowel movements and improve overall digestion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tincture that helps with an upset stomach, you will need a few basic materials. Start by drying and powdering the Curcuma longa roots or turmeric roots. This can be done by spreading the roots thinly on a baking sheet and placing them in a low-temperature oven (around 150-200°F) for 2-3 hours or by air-drying them. Once the powder is ready, add 1 part of the powder to 2 parts of a solvent such as vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Stir the mixture well to ensure the powder is fully dissolved. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the steeping process, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture 2-3 times a day as needed to help alleviate an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for upset stomach

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great help for an upset stomach. One of the key herbs used to soothe stomach issues is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. This herb has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea. It also contains compounds that can help protect the stomach lining from acid damage. Another herb often used to alleviate stomach discomfort is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has natural digestive enzymes that can help break down food and reduce bloating. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease stomach cramps and nausea.  An upset stomach can cause more than just discomfort - it can also lead to missed work, social events, and exercise due to fatigue and pain. If left untreated, stomach issues can also lead to more serious problems like acid reflux, stomach ulcers, and even mental health issues like anxiety and depression. By addressing stomach problems early on, you can improve your quality of life and enjoy activities without the constant worry of stomach discomfort. For example, being able to eat a meal without feeling bloated or nauseous can make a big difference in your daily life. You can enjoy spending time with family and friends, or try new hobbies and exercise routines that you might have previously avoided due to stomach issues. By using herbal tinctures like those containing Glycyrrhiza glabra and Zingiber officinale, you can take the first step towards a healthier and more enjoyable life.
supplementary_best: A combination of Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root) tinctures is often effective in relieving upset stomach. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce nausea. It also has a warming effect that can help stimulate digestion. Licorice root, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties that can help calm the stomach and reduce heartburn. The combination of these two herbs can help to balance digestion and reduce stomach upset. Foeniculum vulgare (fennel) tincture can also be added to this combination as it has carminative properties that can help to ease bloating and gas.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an upset stomach, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. One of these is Wormwood (Artemisia absinthium), which can irritate the stomach and make digestive problems worse. Another is Senna (Cassia senna), a laxative herb that can cause diarrhea, stomach cramps, and dehydration. You should also avoid Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), which can cause stomach upset and interact with other medications. Black Cohosh (Actaea racemosa) may also be problematic, as it can cause stomach upset and interact with blood thinners and other medications. Dandelion root (Taraxacum officinale) can be too stimulating for the stomach and cause digestive issues. Finally, Blue Cohosh (Caulophyllum thalictroides) may cause stomach upset and interact with other medications. It's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal tincture, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition or are taking medications.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help soothe an upset stomach. It contains a compound called aloin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. When consumed, aloe vera gel can also help replace lost fluids and electrolytes, which is especially helpful when you're experiencing vomiting or diarrhea. Some people find relief by eating a small amount of aloe vera gel, usually one to two tablespoons, after meals to ease digestion. Aloe vera is also a natural source of fiber, which can help promote regular bowel movements and prevent constipation.  Another natural remedy for an upset stomach is ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting. It can also help stimulate digestion and relieve cramps. When consumed as a tea or added to food, ginger can help warm the digestive system and promote the breakdown of food. This can be especially helpful for people who experience nausea and vomiting, especially during pregnancy or after surgery.  Probiotics are beneficial bacteria that can help restore the balance of good and bad bacteria in the digestive system. When taken as a supplement or found in fermented foods like yogurt, kefir, and sauerkraut, probiotics can help promote regular bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Probiotics can also help boost the immune system, which can help fight off infections and reduce the severity of digestive issues.  Activated charcoal can also help alleviate an upset stomach. When consumed as a supplement, activated charcoal can help absorb toxins and gas in the digestive system, which can help reduce bloating and discomfort. It can also help alleviate symptoms of IBS and diarrhea by absorbing excess water and electrolytes.  Finally, some people find relief by drinking apple cider vinegar. Apple cider vinegar contains acetic acid, which can help stimulate digestion and promote the breakdown of food. It can also help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of IBS. However, it's essential to dilute the apple cider vinegar with water before consuming it, as undiluted vinegar can be harsh on the digestive system.  These natural remedies can help alleviate an upset stomach by promoting digestion, reducing inflammation, and restoring the balance of good and bad bacteria in the digestive system.

The herb Peppermint can help ease bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing gas. Ginger is another herb that can alleviate bloating, as it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system.  Those with an upset stomach may also suffer from diarrhea, a condition where the body has trouble absorbing nutrients. Slippery Elm can help calm diarrhea by protecting the lining of the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Dandelion root is another herb that can aid in digestion and reduce diarrhea, as it stimulates bowel movements and helps the body absorb nutrients.  Some people with an upset stomach may experience nausea and vomiting, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Licorice root can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation. Chamomile tea is also a natural remedy for nausea, as it calms the digestive system and promotes relaxation.  In addition to these issues, people with an upset stomach may experience stomach cramps and pain. The herb Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the digestive tract. Meadowsweet is another herb that can help alleviate stomach cramps and pain, as it has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.  These herbs can help alleviate symptoms associated with an upset stomach by reducing inflammation, soothing the digestive system, and promoting relaxation.
